repositories.brief_summary: Typechecking speedup for large types and an ephemeron GC fix merged, alongside a ctype scope-error fix and ongoing static-linking discussions.
repositories.new_features_summary: Typechecking speedup for large types (#14900) and ephemeron GC marking fix (#14688) merged, plus a ctype scope bug fix (#14892).
repositories.new_features:
- **Typechecking performance** improved by removing closure allocations from `deep_occur_rec`, which traverses type expressions during unification; benchmarks on a large `js_of_ocaml` file show up to a 45% wall-clock speedup with flambda and 20% without, by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) ([#14900](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14900)), motivated by the regression reported in [#14897](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14897)
- **Major GC ephemeron marking** reworked so `ephe_mark()` calls `mark()` directly on markable data instead of only scheduling it, avoiding quadratic-looking marking behaviour for long ephemeron dependency chains, by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) ([#14688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14688)), a safer follow-up to the previously reverted [#14657](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14657)
- Fixed a **scope error in `ctype`** where non-dependent module-type application could erase the whole result type after the first package type was encountered, causing incorrect types to be accepted for anonymous module type structs, by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([#14892](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14892), fixing [#14891](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14891))
- Removed a useless `Option.map` call in `Btype.fold_row`, a small internal cleanup noticed while working on the typechecking performance PR, by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) ([#14904](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14904))
repositories.activity_summary: New PRs for stdlib additions, large-interval pattern matching, opam macOS support, and a caml_shutdown ephemeron fix; ongoing static-linking work.
repositories.activity:
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) opened **`Dynarray.append_iarray` and `Pqueue.of_iarray`** stdlib additions to avoid copying an `iarray` into a mutable array or resorting to unsafe code ([#14909](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14909), fixing [#14320](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14320))
- [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) opened a fix for **`caml_shutdown()` termination with ephemerons**, addressing an infinite loop in `Phase_sweep_and_mark_main` when the last domain is terminated with `OCAMLRUNPARAM=c=1`, joint work with [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) that has been affecting CI for weeks ([#14906](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14906), related to [#14594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14594))
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened a **pattern-matching compiler** change to support switches over very large integer intervals (beyond the previous $[-2^{16}; 2^{16}]$ limit), with [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) and [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) reviewing edge cases around interval overflow ([#14907](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14907))
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened a fix for **`opam clone` on macOS**, where the default `/bin/sh` (old bash) mishandles heredocs and creates temporary files in the wrong directory, targeted for the 5.5.1 release per [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) ([#14901](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14901), fixing [ocaml/opam-repository#29496](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29496))
- Ongoing design discussion on [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust)'s **static linking and link-time linking parameter resolution** proposal (`-cclib-static`, `-cclib-variant`, `-static`, `-linking-variant`), with [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) requesting a proper design document referencing his own [OCaml library linking RFC](https://github.com/dbuenzli/RFCs/blob/ll-v2/rfcs/ocamlib.md) and [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) noting relevance to MirageOS/Solo5 unikernel toolchains ([#14895](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14895))
- [Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) continued iterating on the **"Signature mismatch" error message rewrite** (renaming "is not included"/"does not include" to subtype/supertype terminology), with extensive wording feedback from [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) and [Brandon Stride](https://github.com/brandonzstride) ([#14896](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14896), following discussion in [#11937](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11937))
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened a **guideline for LLM usage in bug reports**, recommending against mixing LLM-generated content with bug reports and cautioning about LLM-shrunk reproduction cases losing the original bug, reviewed by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole), [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli), [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), and [Miod Vallat](https://github.com/miodvallat) ([#14899](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14899))
- [Anarchos](https://github.com/Sylvain78) continued the **Haiku port** PR covering unix socket tests, single-threaded `setlocale`, and signal handling differences on Haiku, still marked as conflicting ([#14773](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14773))
- [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist)'s PR on **missing minor-heap allocation statistics for the main domain at exit** remains under review, with [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) proposing alternatives to the extra minor-GC-at-exit approach ([#14522](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14522))
- [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist)'s **s390x CFI/backtrace fix**, merged earlier this year, was confirmed and its tracking issue closed this week ([#14500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14500), closing [#13509](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13509))
repositories.discussions_summary: Debate over subtype/supertype wording for the signature-mismatch error message, and pushback on the static-linking proposal's design maturity.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- Extensive back-and-forth on rewording the **"Signature mismatch" compiler error message**, weighing "subtype"/"supertype" terminology against readability and consistency with existing messages about `include`, involving [Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse), [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), and [Brandon Stride](https://github.com/brandonzstride) ([#14896](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14896))
- [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) pushed back on the **static/link-time linking proposal**, arguing that adding new `.cma` metadata fields and a shell-command-based `pkg-config` resolution mechanism needs a design document before merging, citing past problems with ad-hoc `META` file metadata and raising a security concern about archives embedding shell commands run at link time; [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) offered a MirageOS/Solo5 use case as a concrete test ([#14895](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14895))
- Deep investigation into the **typing performance regression** in `js_of_ocaml`'s `lwt_xmlHttpRequest.ml`, with [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue), and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) diagnosing that unification of deeply nested object types causes repeated, non-shared expansion of type abbreviations ([#14897](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14897), related to [#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648) and [#14801](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14801))
- [Simmo Saan](https://github.com/sim642) shared updated **Goblint benchmark results** comparing OCaml 4.14 against 5.6.0+trunk (including [#14885](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14885) and [#14894](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14894)), showing average CPU time regression down to +4% from earlier measurements, prompting [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) to ask about remaining outlier tests ([#13733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13733))
repositories.trends_summary: Continued emphasis on typechecking/GC performance work and increased attention to how LLM-generated content should be handled in bug reports.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- A cluster of **typechecking and GC performance** work landed or progressed this week, including `deep_occur_rec` allocation removal ([#14900](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14900)), ephemeron marking ([#14688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14688)), and ongoing Goblint/Frama-C regression tracking ([#13733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13733), [#11733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11733)), with [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) also reporting `-O3` and inlining-attribute experiments that cut `deep_occur_rec` time further
- Maintainers are formalizing **process around AI-assisted contributions**, with [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)'s bug-report guideline proposal ([#14899](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14899)) following earlier discussion in [#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842)
- Recurring pattern of **closure-allocation elimination in the typechecker** (renamings in recursive definitions from [#14885](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14885)/[#14894](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14894), and `deep_occur_rec` from [#14900](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14900)) as a recurring technique for compile-time speedups, motivated by real-world regressions reported by users of `js_of_ocaml` and Goblint
repositories.issues_summary: A small, self-contained documentation gap in the ocamllex man page is a good starting point for new contributors.
repositories.good_first_issues: - The new `-w` warning-control argument for **`ocamllex`** added in 5.6 is missing from its man page, a small, self-contained documentation fix, reported by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([#14902](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14902))
repositories.contributors_summary: Contributors this week spanned performance engineering, GC internals, error-message design, stdlib additions, and cross-platform build fixes.

repositories.brief_summary: Portability and lock-file bug fixes merged while the tar-based repository performance rewrite (#6625) continued shedding queued sub-PRs.
repositories.new_features_summary: Portability fixes for Solaris/Illumos and MSYS2, lock-file and `config subst` bugfixes, and safer shell hooks merged this week.
repositories.new_features:
- Terminal-resizing (`SIGWINCH`) detection now uses the correct signal number on Solaris/Illumos, where it differs from Linux/macOS/BSD; the fix only applies when opam is built with OCaml >= 5.4 by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([#6933](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6933))
- Windows build fixed for **MSYS2** environments by defining `_WIN32_WINNT` for Windows 8.1 so `GetCurrentProcessToken` is available by [Virgile Robles](https://github.com/Firobe) ([#6862](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6862))
- Lock files whose filter references an undefined variable no longer abort with a fatal error in normal mode (the variable now defaults to `false`), while strict mode now fails gracefully instead of crashing by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) ([#6947](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6947), fixes [#6946](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6946))
- Shell integration hooks for `zsh`, `bash`, `sh`, and `fish` now explicitly `return 0` so a failed `opam env` sourcing no longer leaks a stale error code into the user's shell prompt by [Talel Zighni](https://github.com/ta2005) ([#6930](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6930))
- `opam config subst` now correctly handles absolute pathnames instead of erroring with `File_not_found` by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) ([#6936](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6936), fixes [#6925](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6925))
- The opam 1.2-to-2.0 repository upgrade process no longer downloads packages without checksums just to record an untrusted MD5, removing a source of non-reproducible test/CI results by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([#6978](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6978))
- `opam repo add` and `opam init` no longer fail the first time they scan a repository's `packages` directory if it contains a subdirectory literally named `opam` — a regression that previously only worked around itself on a subsequent `opam update`, by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([#6995](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6995), detected in [#6941](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6941))
- `OpamSystem`'s `open_in`-family functions now error consistently on directories across Unix and Windows instead of silently opening them on Unix, alongside new debug logging distinguishing repository loads from a directory versus a diff, by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) ([#6941](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6941))
- Documentation: the packaging guide now recommends using **static archives** for a package's `src:` URL, since forge-generated archives (e.g. GitHub's) can change over time and break checksums recorded in opam-repository, by [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) ([#6973](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6973))
repositories.activity_summary: Five new PRs opened remain in review; the repository-tarring rewrite (#6625) kept merging queued sub-PRs and dev-meeting triage cleared several long-standing issues.
repositories.activity:
- Five PRs opened this week remain under review: silencing warnings raised by the upcoming OCaml 5.6 by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([#6987](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6987)), a fix for `opam lock`'s handling of dependency-formula disjunctions by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([#6990](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6990), fixes [#6944](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6944)), an effort to make opam's git invocations deterministic regardless of the user's global/system git config by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([#6992](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6992), fixes [#6937](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6937)), a draft so `OpamFilename.open_in` no longer opens directories on Unix by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) ([#6996](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6996), fixes [#6997](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6997)), and a draft memoisation change to `OpamRepositoryRoot` tgz folding extracted from the larger repository rewrite by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) ([#7001](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/7001))
- The long-running **repository-as-archive** rewrite of `opam update`/`opam init` ([#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate), aiming to replace repeated untar/rescan cycles with reading the `tar.gz` directly via `ocaml-tar`, kept shedding queued sub-PRs this week: [#6941](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6941), [#6995](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6995), [#6998](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6998), and [#7002](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/7002) merged, while [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) opened [#6994](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6994) to abstract `OpamAdminRepoUpgrade.do_upgrade` for layouts other than a plain directory
- CI stabilization saw several merges: `actions/cache` bumped from 5 to 6 by [Dependabot](https://github.com/dependabot) ([#6983](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6983)), the depexts workflow fixed by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([#6972](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6972)), and the doc/depends jobs updated to handle an opam-root format upgrade by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) ([#6998](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6998), [#7002](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/7002))
- Continued discussion on [Dmitri Volkov](https://github.com/sporkl)'s `opam source --dev` git-config PR ([#6955](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6955), fixes [#6451](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6451)) explored renaming the new argument from `for_source` to `no_config` and back, and whether `opam source` should switch to `git clone` outright to also fix branch-fetching problems surfaced by [#6992](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6992)
- [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb)'s fix for `OpamFilename.starts_with`/`dir_starts_with` incorrectly treating `foo/bar` as a prefix of `foo/bar-baz` ([#6953](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6953), fixes [#6948](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6948)) went through review of `OpamFilename.t`'s implicit invariants, prompting [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) to open [#6986](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6986) proposing a full rewrite of `OpamFilename`
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) proposed workarounds for `rsync` mishandling symlinks in local opam repositories, surfaced by caching an `_opam` switch directory between CI runs ([#6977](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6977))
- [Kayo Tei](https://github.com/tuesdayjz)'s PR to skip default-compiler selection during `opam init --bare`, saving several seconds of startup time, remains open ([#6957](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6957), fixes [#5815](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5815))
- [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb)'s PR to prompt users to remove auto-pinned packages after a failed `opam install` remains open, queued on [#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910) ([#6923](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6923))
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened a draft fixing filename-argument parsing so `/` is recognised as a directory separator on Windows ([#6981](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6981), fixes [#6940](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6940))
- A GHA upgrade to build against OCaml 5.5 and 4.14.4 was opened, blocked on an upstream issue in ocsigen/lwt before it could merge ([#6988](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6988), blocked by [ocsigen/lwt#1081](https://github.com/ocsigen/lwt/issues/1081))
- [Guilherme FACCINI BANDEIRA](https://github.com/faccinib)'s manpage-highlighting homogenisation PR remains under review ([#6921](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6921), fixes [#6112](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6112))
repositories.discussions_summary: Dev-meeting triage addressed several long-standing bugs, including stale state caches, non-deterministic `opam update`, and Windows DLL loading.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) reported that a stale repository state cache after an opam upgrade causes slow `--safe` operations (and slow editor startup via `opam list --safe`); [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) linked it to [#6799](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6799) and [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened [ocaml-opam/opam-user-setup#64](https://github.com/ocaml-opam/opam-user-setup/issues/64) to check `stderr` handling before adding a warning ([#6975](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6975))
- Debugging of [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok)'s `opam update` patch-application failure ([#6937](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6937)) traced the root cause to `git diff.renames=copies` in the user's `.gitconfig`; [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) pointed to a fix in [hannesm/patch#44](https://github.com/hannesm/patch/pull/44), and [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened [#6992](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6992) to make opam's git invocations ignore user/system git config
- Dev meeting discussion on [Austin Theriault](https://github.com/ajbt200128)'s request to switch opam's GitHub releases to immutable releases concluded it is too restrictive; instead the team plans to document GPG signature verification of releases more prominently ([#6900](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6900))
- Dev meeting discussion on `opam-installer`'s dependency cycle through `opam-core`/jsonm ([#6907](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6907)) found no trivial solution; splitting out a dependency-less `opam-core0` package was floated but risks over-complicating the codebase
- Dev meeting concluded that DLL loading failures for opam plugins depending on depexts-installed libraries on Windows ([#6931](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6931)) should be fixed by adding the relevant cygwin paths to `opam env`'s path updates
- A 2018-era report of unreliable Ctrl-C handling during package installation ([#3709](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/3709)) was revisited in dev meeting, which pointed at wildcard exception handlers swallowing `Sys.Break` and proposed auditing the ~20 occurrences of catch-all exception handling in the codebase
- Dev meeting confirmed [Simmo Saan](https://github.com/sim642)'s report that opam lock files don't use disjunctions for transitive dependencies shared between `with-doc` and `with-dev-setup` is a genuine bug, to be fixed by detecting overlapping variable filters ([#6935](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6935))
- [Olivier Andrieu](https://github.com/oandrieu)'s report of a menhir upgrade failure on Windows caused by case-sensitivity mismatches between `lib\menhirLib` and `lib\menhirlib` directories was discussed in dev meeting, with the team agreeing they first need to reproduce it ([#6909](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6909))
repositories.trends_summary: Dev-meeting triage cleared a backlog of long-standing bugs while portability fixes and lock-file hardening continued as recurring themes.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Recurring "discussed in dev meeting" triage cleared verdicts on several long-standing issues this week ([#6900](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6900), [#6907](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6907), [#6931](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6931), [#6935](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6935), [#3709](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/3709), [#6909](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6909)), suggesting a regular backlog-grooming process led by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou)

repositories.brief_summary: Documentation fix for the typeSearch JSON schema and removal of temporary Merlin version pins; two draft PRs closed after review feedback.
repositories.new_features_summary: Removed temporary Merlin version pins now that 5.5 support shipped, and corrected a malformed JSON example in the typeSearch docs.
repositories.new_features:
- Removed the temporary `merlin-lib` and `ocaml-index` version pins from `ocaml-lsp-server.opam` and `ocaml-lsp-server.opam.template` now that **Merlin 5.5 support** has been released upstream, simplifying installation from source for users by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) ([9e60393](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/commit/9e60393bccad65e64e5d1db48a5e573e381ca003))
- Corrected a malformed **JSON response example** in the `ocamllsp/typeSearch` documentation, where the schema incorrectly nested an array inside an object instead of an array of objects, by [Clément Delafargue](https://github.com/divarvel) ([#1613](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1613))
repositories.activity_summary: Two draft PRs opened and closed after review feedback on diagnostic message formatting and dependency pinning; automated flake.lock PR remains open.
repositories.activity:
- [Pedro Castro](https://github.com/aspeddro) opened a PR pinning `merlin-lib` and `ocaml-index` to `5.8-505` to work around an opam switch resolution failure ([#1611](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1611)), closed after [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) noted the pin was no longer necessary following the Merlin release and removed it directly on `master`
- [Pedro Castro](https://github.com/aspeddro) also opened a draft PR reverting `Diagnostic.message` to a plain string instead of accepting markup content ([#1612](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1612)), closed after [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) pointed out that markdown-formatted diagnostic messages are allowed as of **LSP 3.18**
- [Clément Delafargue](https://github.com/divarvel) opened and merged a documentation typo fix for the `typeSearch` JSON response schema ([#1613](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1613))
- The automated **flake.lock update** PR from the `github-actions` bot remains open, tracking bumps to the `merlin5_3` and `nixpkgs` flake inputs ([#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437))
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) updated e2e **hover test expectations** on `master` to reflect the type-alias resolution behavior merged the previous week in [#1579](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1579) ([329c4d6](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/commit/329c4d684a4358398795a6e317052605c88c3e80))
repositories.discussions_summary: Reviewers clarified that markdown diagnostics are supported since LSP 3.18 and that the Merlin version pins were no longer needed post-release.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- On [#1612](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1612), [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) clarified that **markdown-formatted diagnostic messages** have been allowed by the LSP specification since version 3.18, leading [Pedro Castro](https://github.com/aspeddro) to close the draft PR that would have reverted diagnostics to plain strings
- On [#1611](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1611), [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) explained that pinning `merlin-lib` and `ocaml-index` was no longer required immediately after the release, and removed the pin directly on `master` instead of merging the PR

repositories.brief_summary: Kate closed two tracking issues on OCaml 5.5 support and branch staleness while Ulysse's LRU indexing cache PR saw active review.
repositories.activity_summary: Dependabot opened a GitHub Actions cache bump while Ulysse's LRU cache indexing PR received extensive review feedback from art-w.
repositories.activity:
- A **dependency update PR** bumping `actions/cache` from 4 to 6 was opened by [Dependabot](https://github.com/dependabot) ([#2085](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2085)), effectively superseding an earlier bump from 4 to 5 that was closed without merging the same day ([#2013](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2013)).
- The **LRU cache for indexing memory usage** PR by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) continued under review, with benchmark results showing index aggregation running roughly twice as fast as before the underlying pointing-indexes work, alongside a modest increase in on-disk index size ([#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2079), building on prior work in [#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)).
- Iterative fixes were pushed to the **union-find and caching logic** underlying the indexing PR in response to review, including switching the cache-size flag's unit to kilobytes with a 1GB default and factoring out duplicate cache-lookup logic ([#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2079)).
repositories.discussions_summary: Ulysse and art-w debated union-find correctness, cache eviction, and on-disk schema handling in the LRU indexing PR.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) raised detailed questions about the **functional union-find implementation** in the LRU cache PR, including whether `Uid_set.union` needed to be recomputed when `ensure` terminates early and whether the `find_and_compress` helper could return the discovered root directly instead of creating a new link ([#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2079)).
- Discussion on **on-disk index size** examined whether a 2MB increase was caused by the newly added `pos: int option` field, with [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) sharing size comparisons across the before/pointing-indexes/LRU variants to isolate the cause ([#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2079)).
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) also questioned whether the cache-eviction path was missing a `Cache.replace` call to record entries for reuse, and whether the `Serialized_reused` status was still needed given it appeared redundant with `Serialized` ([#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2079)).
repositories.trends_summary: Continued investment in indexer memory/performance tuning alongside routine CI dependency maintenance and OCaml 5.5 readiness tracking.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Ongoing **memory and performance tuning of the indexer** through the LRU cache work, following a pattern of incremental optimization after the earlier pointing-indexes changes it builds on ([#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2079), [#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)).
- Routine **CI dependency upkeep** via Dependabot-managed GitHub Actions bumps continues on a regular cadence, with older bump PRs closed as newer ones supersede them ([#2085](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2085), [#2013](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2013)).
- Closure of the long-running **OCaml 5.5 support tracking issue** and the **504-branch staleness report** by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) suggests progress on compiler-version compatibility ahead of the 5.5 freeze ([#2024](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2024), [#1937](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1937)).
repositories.contributors_summary: Ulysse authored the LRU cache indexing PR, art-w provided in-depth review, and Kate closed two tracking issues.
repositories.contributors:
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) authored the **LRU cache for indexing memory usage** PR, ran and reported benchmark comparisons for build and aggregation performance and index size, and pushed follow-up commits addressing review feedback ([#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2079)).
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) reviewed the LRU cache PR in depth, asking about union-find correctness, cache eviction edge cases, schema/status tracking, and the file-size implications of the new on-disk representation ([#2079](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2079)).
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) closed the **504 branch out-of-date** report and the **OCaml 5.5 support** tracking issue ([#1937](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1937), [#2024](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2024)).
- [Dependabot](https://github.com/dependabot) opened and closed routine GitHub Actions dependency bump PRs for `actions/cache` ([#2085](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2085), [#2013](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2013)).

repositories.brief_summary: Dune 3.24.0 shipped this week, quickly followed by fixes for melange sandboxing, formatter env vars, and root_module regressions.
repositories.new_features_summary: Watch-mode status line gained build duration/parallelism and trace timing, dune tools exec now supports utop/odoc, and C compiler vendor detection was aligned with OCaml's.
repositories.new_features:
- **Build duration and parallelism** are now shown in the watch-mode status line, tracking process time against wall-clock time to display an average parallelism multiplier ([#15384](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15384), [#15390](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15390)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg).
- Build trace events now record **process counts and CPU/wall-clock timing totals** (elapsed, user CPU, system CPU) alongside the existing build-finish event, improving profiling of what a build actually spent time on ([#15401](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15401)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg).
- `dune tools exec` now supports **`utop` and `odoc`** in addition to the other dev tools, making the interface uniform across all installable tools ([#15388](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15388)) by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV).
- **C compiler vendor detection** was realigned with the identifiers used by the OCaml compiler itself (`msvc`, `icc`, `mingw`, `clang`, `gcc`, `xlc`, `sunc`), removing spurious "please open an issue" warnings for previously-unrecognized compilers; `mingw-clang` now links against `-lstdc++` instead of `-lc++` ([#15423](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15423), fixes [#14870](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14870)) by [Chizy](https://github.com/chizy7).
- Fixed `dune utop` **crashing on absolute paths** to workspace directories, where `dune utop $PWD/lib` raised an internal `Code_error` while `dune utop lib` worked ([#15272](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15272), fixes [#15108](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15108)) by [Chizy](https://github.com/chizy7).
- Default **sandboxing for Melange rules was reverted** after it was found to dominate build time in libraries with many modules, adding significant sandbox creation/destruction overhead ([#15418](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15418)) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro).
- Fixed a **missing source dependency** in the sandbox when preprocessing Melange sources, which could cause stale or missing files during preprocessing ([#15395](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15395), regression test in [#15393](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15393)) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro).
- Fixed **environment variables not being passed to formatter actions**, a regression that broke formatters like `refmt` which read configuration (e.g. print width) from the environment ([#15386](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15386), regression test in [#15385](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15385)) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro).
- The `root_module` field **no longer pulls in ppx runtime dependencies**; such dependencies now need to be declared explicitly in `libraries` ([#15419](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15419)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg).
- Fixed typo `Disabed` → `Disabled` in `dune_config_file.ml` user-facing config output ([#15410](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15410)) by [Hugo Herbelin](https://github.com/herbelin).
- Updated developer-tool documentation to reflect that `dune tools exec` now covers `odoc` and `utop` ([#15404](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15404)) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder).
repositories.activity_summary: Dune 3.24.0 was released and closed its tracker, while newly opened work covers Rocq packaging, Melange sandbox staging, and Nix aarch64 builds.
repositories.activity:
- **Dune 3.24.0 was released** this week, merging the release branch and closing its tracker issue ([#14621](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14621), [#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14620)) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder); within days, regressions in Melange sandboxing, formatter environment variables, and Melange preprocessing were found and flagged as candidates for a follow-up patch release.
- Newly opened work includes [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) staging wrapped root dependencies for JS emission in the Melange sandbox ([#15376](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15376)), [Rodolphe Lepigre](https://github.com/rlepigre-skylabs-ai) adding preliminary support for **Rocq's packaging support** ([#15400](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15400)), [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS) opening a documentation page explaining **shell completion** ([#15403](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15403)), and [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) opening PRs to use an **Aarch64 toolchain** and consistent compiler settings for the `dune-static` Nix build ([#15407](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15407), [#15408](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15408)).
- Older open work continues, including [Javier Chávarri](https://github.com/jchavarri)'s progress-display counters and elapsed-time PR ([#13263](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13263), partially superseded by this week's [#15384](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15384)), [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)'s work to shorten shared jsoo runtime paths ([#14768](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14768)), [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)'s odoc build-dir cmti fallback fix ([#15293](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15293)) and pending-promotion completion for shell completion ([#15338](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15338)), and [Rodolphe Lepigre](https://github.com/rlepigre-skylabs-ai)'s Rocq output-test placement and naming PRs ([#15347](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15347), [#15349](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15349)).
- The **Melange sandboxing regression** was resolved through a sequence of test-then-fix PRs: a regression test showing wrapped root interface deps missing from the sandbox ([#15375](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15375)), a `deps_of_closure` reuse refactor ([#15377](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15377)), a regression test for the ppx sandbox issue ([#15393](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15393)), the fix itself ([#15395](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15395)), and finally reverting default sandboxing altogether due to its performance cost ([#15418](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15418)), all by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro).
- The **RPC long-poll subsystem** was refactored and hardened: moving `Long_poll` into its own module ([#15369](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15369)), tracking pollers with an `Active_set` ([#15378](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15378)), and adding tests for idle long-poll state retention and batch shutdown with a monitor client ([#15415](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15415), [#15366](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15366)), all by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg).
- The **watch-mode status line** work went through several iterations: an earlier restart-status simplification and elapsed-time-counting attempt were closed without merging ([#14360](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14360), [#15389](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15389)) before landing as build duration and parallelism display ([#15384](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15384), [#15390](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15390)), alongside a scheduler fix to reap subreaper children on an isolated loop during shutdown ([#15383](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15383)) and a test covering cached failed actions ([#15414](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15414)).
- The **formatter environment-variable regression** was caught and fixed with a paired regression test and fix ([#15385](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15385), [#15386](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15386)) plus a changelog entry ([#15394](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15394)) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro), while a related `dune fmt`/CI formatting fix landed to stop applying diffs silently when `--preview` is used ([#15379](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15379), [#15380](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15380)) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter).
- The `--auto-promote` **exit-code fix was reverted** after it caused a `dune fmt` re-run regression, with [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) taking over to find an alternative fix ([#15397](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15397), tracked in [#785](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/785)).
- Cram-test reliability work landed, including sanitizing partial output from timed-out cram tests ([#15354](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15354)) and removing sleeps from timeout tests ([#15416](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15416)), by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg).
- Package-management cram tests were extended for undeclared-package PATH resolution and program-resolution narrowing ([#15222](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15222), [#15412](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15412), [#15413](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15413)) by [Puneeth Chaganti](https://github.com/punchagan), who also unblocked a stuck mirage CI workflow ([#15273](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15273)).
- Miscellaneous refactors landed in `stdune` and the action-execution path, including moving action types into `stdune` ([#15391](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15391)), a labelled `Option.value_exn'` for clearer `Code_error` diagnostics ([#15396](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15396)), cosmetic cleanups to `action_exec` ([#15398](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15398)), and removal of unused `inter_all` from `dir_set` ([#15421](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15421)), by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg).
- CI dependency maintenance updated `actions/checkout` and `actions/cache` to newer versions ([#15373](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15373), [#15374](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15374)) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro), alongside a Dependabot-proposed `actions/cache` bump that was closed without merging ([#15372](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15372)).
- A **pull request template** was added to standardize PR descriptions ([#15399](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15399)) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder).
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion focused on release cadence for outstanding regressions and design questions around dev-tool invocation and package-context resolution.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- On the **3.24.0 release tracker**, [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) flagged that the formatter env-var regression ([#15386](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15386)) needed a follow-up patch release since 3.24.0 had already been published to opam; [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) confirmed the fix would go into a patch or the next minor release ([#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14620)).
- On a long-standing ocaml-re regression issue, [v-gb](https://github.com/v-gb) asked [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) about dune's release cadence, noting it appeared to be roughly every two months and asking whether a release was imminent, since the regression was causing recurring CI failures downstream ([#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14775)).
- The motivation for supporting `dune tools exec utop` was debated at length: [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) questioned why it was needed given `dune utop` already exists, and [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) explained the internal Tarides discussion that led to wanting a uniform interface across all installed dev tools, with [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) agreeing a bare, uniform `utop` invocation has value alongside the project-aware `dune utop` ([#15388](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15388)).
- Reviewing the pending-promotion completion PR, [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) and [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS) worked through why `dune promote` doesn't recognize the same flags as `dune promotion apply`, and why completion doesn't activate for non-`dune`-named binaries like `_boot/dune.exe`, tracing the root cause to an old cmdliner completion issue ([#15338](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15338), referencing [#6160](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/6160)).
- [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) proposed catching the exception in `load_user_config_file` to give a clearer error message when `~/.config/dune` is a regular file rather than a directory, noting the crash is a regression versus dune 3.21.0 ([#15406](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15406)).
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) questioned whether the C compiler vendor detection change had user-visible effects; [Chizy](https://github.com/chizy7) clarified that only `xlc`/`sunc` (no longer warn) and `mingw-clang` (now links `-lstdc++` instead of `-lc++`) are affected ([#15423](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15423)).
repositories.trends_summary: Regressions are being caught within days of release via a consistent test-then-fix workflow, alongside a push for richer watch-mode build observability.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- A **test-then-fix workflow** recurs across the week's merges: regression tests land first and the actual fix follows in a separate PR, seen with the Melange preprocessing bug ([#15393](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15393) → [#15395](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15395)), the formatter env-var bug ([#15385](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15385) → [#15386](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15386)), and the `dune fmt` re-run bug ([#15379](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15379) → [#15397](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15397)).
- **Post-release regression response** was fast: three separate regressions in Melange sandboxing, formatter environment handling, and preprocessing dependencies were all identified and fixed within days of the 3.24.0 release going out ([#15418](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15418), [#15386](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15386), [#15395](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15395)), with explicit discussion of bundling them into a patch release ([#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14620)).
- **Watch-mode build observability** continues to expand, with build duration, parallelism, and now process-level CPU/wall-clock trace totals all landing this week ([#15384](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15384), [#15390](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15390), [#15401](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15401)), building on the still-open progress-counters PR ([#13263](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13263)).
- **RPC long-poll robustness** is being invested in independently of the release cycle, with a module split, `Active_set`-based poller tracking, and new tests for idle retention and batch shutdown all landing this week ([#15369](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15369), [#15378](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15378), [#15415](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15415), [#15366](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15366)).
repositories.issues_summary: Approachable issues cover a CLI exit-code regression, incorrect runtest documentation, and a config-file crash with a proposed fix.
repositories.good_first_issues:
- **Exit with code 0 when using `--auto-promote`**, labeled `help wanted`, is being reworked after an earlier fix caused a regression; a fresh approach is needed ([#785](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/785)).
- The **`runtest` documentation is incorrect for projects with multiple contexts**, a self-contained documentation fix ([#15405](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15405)) reported by [Simmo Saan](https://github.com/sim642).
- **Compilation fails when `~/.config/dune` is a regular file** instead of a directory; [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) already proposed a concrete fix (catching the exception in `load_user_config_file` and showing a clearer message), making this a good starting point for a new contributor ([#15406](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15406)).
repositories.contributors_summary: Contributions spanned watch-mode observability, RPC hardening, Melange regression fixes, C compiler detection, and package-management test coverage.
repositories.contributors:
- [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) implemented watch-mode build duration and parallelism display ([#15384](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15384), [#15390](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15390)), build process trace timing ([#15401](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15401)), the RPC long-poll module refactor and `Active_set` poller tracking ([#15369](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15369), [#15378](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15378)), a scheduler shutdown fix ([#15383](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15383)), the `root_module` ppx-dependency fix ([#15417](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15417), [#15419](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15419)), several stdune/action-exec refactors ([#15391](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15391), [#15396](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15396), [#15398](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15398), [#15421](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15421)), cram-test reliability fixes ([#15354](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15354), [#15416](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15416)), and reviewed the C compiler vendor detection PR ([#15423](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15423)).

- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) managed the 3.24.0 release and closed its tracker ([#14621](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14621), [#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14620)), added a pull request template ([#15399](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15399)), updated dev-tool documentation ([#15404](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15404)), fixed a stuck mirage CI workflow ([#15273](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15273)), and reviewed the formatter and melange regression fixes, prompting changelog entries for both ([#15386](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15386), [#15395](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15395)).

- [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) reported the C compiler vendor detection issue ([#14870](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14870)), reverted the `--auto-promote` exit-code fix after it caused a regression and wrote the regression test that surfaced it ([#15397](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15397), [#15379](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15379)), fixed a CI formatting check ([#15380](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15380)), and reviewed the odoc build-dir fallback and shell-completion promotion PRs ([#15293](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15293), [#15338](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15338)).
- [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) added `dune tools exec utop` support and explained the design rationale in review discussion ([#15388](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15388)), and opened PRs for an Aarch64 toolchain and consistent compiler settings for the `dune-static` Nix build ([#15407](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15407), [#15408](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15408)).

- [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247) investigated the mdx `runtest --force` bug and confirmed it was already fixed upstream ([#13418](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13418)), is exploring exposing the lock-directory dependency tree ([#15382](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15382)), and reviewed the `dune fmt` regression test and CI formatting fix ([#15379](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15379), [#15380](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15380)).
- [Rodolphe Lepigre](https://github.com/rlepigre-skylabs-ai) opened PRs to attach Rocq output tests to the right directory, alias Rocq output test names, and add preliminary support for Rocq's packaging support ([#15347](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15347), [#15349](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15349), [#15400](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15400)).
- [Hugo Herbelin](https://github.com/herbelin) fixed a typo in `dune_config_file.ml` ([#15410](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15410)).
- [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) reported and proposed a fix for a crash when `~/.config/dune` is a regular file ([#15406](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15406)).
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) reviewed the C compiler vendor detection PR, asking about user-visible effects, and flagged a missing changelog/manual entry on the `foreign_library` `c_library_flags` PR ([#15423](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15423), [#13484](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13484)).

repositories.brief_summary: Gabriel Radanne opened an RFC proposing a compiler-assisted API for manipulating destinations (write-once pointers) (#62)
repositories.new_features_summary: A draft RFC document proposing an API to expose and manipulate destinations for PPXs was added (#62)
repositories.new_features: - New draft **RFC document** `rfcs/exposed_dest.md` proposing a **compiler-assisted API for destinations** (write-once pointers into allocated blocks), aimed at making destination-passing-style transformations such as tail-modulo-cons implementable as PPXs rather than requiring compiler support, opened by [Gabriel Radanne](https://github.com/Drup) ([#62](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/62))
repositories.activity_summary: The week's only activity was the opening of RFC #62 on exposing destinations, with no comments or merges yet
repositories.activity: - A **new RFC PR was opened** by [Gabriel Radanne](https://github.com/Drup) proposing to expose destinations (write-once pointers used in tail-modulo-cons and related destination-passing-style transformations) so they can be manipulated from PPXs rather than only from the compiler's internal Lambda IR ([#62](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/62)); the proposal remains open and awaiting review, with no comments yet recorded
repositories.contributors_summary: Gabriel Radanne authored and opened the week's sole RFC proposal on exposing destinations
repositories.contributors: - [Gabriel Radanne](https://github.com/Drup) authored and opened a new RFC proposing a compiler-assisted API for exposing and manipulating destinations, covering motivation from tail-modulo-cons transformations and related destination-passing-style use cases ([#62](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/62))

repositories.brief_summary: OxCaml support for `include functor` and modes advanced in review while a portable `grep` fix for BSD test compatibility merged.
repositories.new_features_summary: Test suite now invokes `grep` in a BSD/OpenBSD-portable way, fixing OpenBSD CI failures ([#1453](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1453)).
repositories.new_features: - Fixed portable invocation of `grep` across the test suite, switching from `grep <term> -B 2` to `grep -B2 <term>` so BSD/OpenBSD's non-GNU `grep` (which requires options before the search term and rejects spaced short options) can parse the commands correctly, resolving OpenBSD CI failures that followed the reference documentation work in [#1442](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1442); by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) ([#1453](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1453))
repositories.activity_summary: OxCaml compatibility work continued with two large open PRs adding `include functor` display and mode rendering support.
repositories.activity:
- Two substantial **OxCaml compatibility** PRs remain open for review: [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) added support for displaying `include functor` inclusions, a follow-up to [#1368](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1368) ([#1452](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1452)), and [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) implemented rendering of OxCaml **modes**, including heuristics ported from the OxCaml compiler to omit modes that are legacy, implied, or inferable from context, fixing [#1417](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1417) ([#1454](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1454))
- [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) also merged the `grep` portability fix for the test suite, following up on [#1442](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1442) ([#1453](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1453))
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) opened [#1455](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1455) reporting a `Failure("invalid core type")` exception when loading `.cmti` files for the `base` library under OxCaml, and traced it in the comments to a `Ptyp_constr` occurring inside a `[@@deriving globalize, sexp_of]`-annotated type combining `immutable_data with ...` and a `Comparator.t` constraint in `map_intf.ml`
- [Benjamin Canou](https://github.com/klakplok) opened [#1456](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1456) describing spurious clickable links in rendered source pages (e.g. on `char.ml.html` in the `core` library docs) caused by `ppx_deriving`-generated code with mislocated, non-ghost AST nodes that make the HTML source renderer emit nested, auto-closing `<a>` tags around unrelated tokens
- [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) continued discussion on [#1450](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1450) about `odoc_driver` resolving same-named modules from sibling libraries (Eliom's `eliom.client` and `eliom.server`) to the wrong library; investigation points to `base_args` in `src/driver/odoc_units_of.ml` adding every package library to each unit's `-L` search path, with a narrower link-path patch referenced at [#1449](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1449) discussed as not suitable as a global default
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) followed up on the long-standing [#1419](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1419) request to document `include functor module type of ...` inclusions, pointing to the proposed fix in [#1452](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1452)
repositories.discussions_summary: Detailed root-cause investigations into module resolution and source-link rendering bugs stood out this week.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) and [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) discussed how `odoc_driver`'s link path construction in `src/driver/odoc_units_of.ml` causes same-named modules in sibling libraries (Eliom's client/server split) to resolve to the wrong library, weighing a narrower per-library link path against the package-wide reference availability `odoc_driver` intentionally provides ([#1450](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1450), referencing patch [#1449](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1449))
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) traced a `cmti`-loading crash on OxCaml builds of `base` to a specific `Ptyp_constr` inside a jkind-annotated type declaration in `map_intf.ml`, isolating the reproduction to `read_jkind_annotation` in `src/loader/cmi.ml` ([#1455](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1455))
- [Benjamin Canou](https://github.com/klakplok) laid out a detailed hypothesis for spurious source-link rendering, tracing it to `ppx_deriving`-generated ASTs with non-ghost, mislocated nodes reaching `Typedtree_traverse`, and suggested comparing parsed and actual source locations as a possible mitigation in the HTML source renderer ([#1456](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1456))
repositories.trends_summary: OxCaml compatibility (include functor, modes, jkind annotations) remains the dominant theme, alongside continued cross-platform test hardening.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Continued investment in **OxCaml compatibility**, spanning both large feature PRs for `include functor` display and mode rendering ([#1452](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1452), [#1454](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1454)) and bug reports surfaced by real-world OxCaml builds such as jkind-annotated type parsing failures in `base` ([#1455](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1455))
- Ongoing **cross-platform test suite hardening** for non-Linux/non-GNU environments, with the `grep` portability fix ([#1453](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1453)) following directly from the prior week's reference documentation portability work ([#1442](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1442))
- Recurring reports of **module resolution edge cases** in link generation, from sibling libraries with shared module names ([#1450](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1450)) to ppx-generated code confusing source-link boundaries ([#1456](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1456))
repositories.contributors_summary: Six contributors were active this week through OxCaml feature PRs, a portability fix, detailed bug investigations, and issue discussions.
repositories.contributors:
- [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) opened a large PR adding `include functor` display support for OxCaml, a follow-up to [#1368](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1368) ([#1452](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1452)), and authored and merged the portable `grep` fix for the test suite, following up on [#1442](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1442) ([#1453](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1453))
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) opened a PR implementing rendering of OxCaml modes, including heuristics to omit legacy or inferable modes, fixing [#1417](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1417) ([#1454](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1454))
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) reported and investigated a `cmti`-loading crash affecting OxCaml builds of `base`, tracing the fault to a specific type declaration pattern in the comments ([#1455](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1455)), and reviewed the `grep` portability fix ([#1453](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1453))
- [Benjamin Canou](https://github.com/klakplok) reported spurious clickable links in rendered source pages and provided a detailed root-cause hypothesis involving `ppx_deriving`-generated ASTs ([#1456](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1456))
- [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) reported and discussed module resolution issues affecting sibling libraries with shared module names (Eliom's client/server split), sharing a patch and investigation findings ([#1450](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1450))
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) followed up on a documentation-display request for `include functor` inclusions, linking it to the proposed fix ([#1419](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1419), [#1452](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/1452))

repositories.brief_summary: OCaml Workshop 2026 deadline dates corrected on the site, a broken cookbook link reported, and a dune 3.24 announcement PR opened.
repositories.new_features_summary: OCaml Workshop 2026 page corrected to show 12 Jul submission deadline and 3 Aug notification date.
repositories.new_features:
- Fixed the **OCaml Workshop 2026 "Important Dates" panel**, updating the submission deadline pill from `1 Jul` to `12 Jul` and the speaker-notification pill from `27 Jul` to `3 Aug` in `ocaml_workshop_2026.eml` ([#3707](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3707), [#3708](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3708))
- Ingested five new **OCaml Planet blog posts** via automated scraping, including [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/anil)'s `.plan-26-26` update, [Mark Elvers](https://github.com/tunbury)'s notes on an OpenBSD CI worker copy-corruption bug and a weekly summary post, [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/ocsigen)'s writeup on why Ocsigen is staying on Lwt instead of switching to Eio, and the 30 Jun edition of OCaml Weekly News ([#3702](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3702), [#3705](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3705), [#3706](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3706))
repositories.activity_summary: A dune 3.24 release announcement PR remains open pending merge, alongside a still-open platform releases scrape PR.
repositories.activity:
- A **dune 3.24 release announcement** was opened by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), cc'ing [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) for review, and remains unmerged at week's end ([#3704](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3704))
- An automated **platform releases scrape PR** adding 212 lines across four files has been open since before this week and is still awaiting merge ([#3693](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3693))
- [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247) landed two follow-up corrections to the **OCaml Workshop 2026 deadlines**, first adjusting the dates and then refining them again to their final values ([#3707](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3707), [#3708](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3708))
- Routine **automated content ingestion** continued via three merged Planet scrape PRs bringing in new blog posts and OCaml Weekly News editions ([#3702](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3702), [#3705](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3705), [#3706](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3706))
- A **broken link in the Dream web-server cookbook** was reported, pointing to a stale `aantron.github.io/dream` URL that should redirect to the project's new `camlworks.github.io/dream` home ([#3703](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3703))
repositories.discussions_summary: A new contributor's attempt to fix a broken cookbook link surfaced a local build failure in the opam switch setup.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [Tibor](https://github.com/Geerayef) reported the broken Dream cookbook link and then attempted to submit the fix themselves, but hit a build failure in `make switch` where `topkg` failed to compile because an unrelated local opam switch named `coi` was being referenced instead of the `default` switch — the thread documents the exact opam/dune error output for maintainers to help diagnose ([#3703](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3703))
repositories.trends_summary: Recurring maintenance (event-date corrections, automated content scraping) dominated a quiet week with light discussion activity.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Site content this week was driven mainly by **recurring automated ingestion** (Planet blog scraping, platform release tracking) rather than new manual authoring, consistent with prior weeks' pattern of scrape PRs forming the bulk of merged changes ([#3702](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3702), [#3705](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3705), [#3706](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3706), [#3693](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3693))
- **Event logistics upkeep** continued for the OCaml Workshop 2026 page, with a second date correction following shortly after the first, suggesting the deadline details were still being finalised with organisers ([#3707](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3707), [#3708](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3708))
- The **local dev environment setup** (`make switch`/opam) surfaced as a barrier for at least one prospective contributor attempting a small documentation fix, an onboarding friction point worth tracking ([#3703](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3703))
repositories.issues_summary: A simple, unlabeled broken-link fix in the Dream cookbook is approachable for a new contributor once the local build issue is resolved.
repositories.good_first_issues: - Fixing the **stale Dream documentation link** in the cookbook (replacing `aantron.github.io/dream/#templates` with `camlworks.github.io/dream/#templates`) is a small, self-contained content fix suitable for a new contributor, though the reporter's own attempt was blocked by a local `make switch` build failure that a maintainer may need to help resolve first ([#3703](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3703))
repositories.contributors_summary: Sudha Parimala corrected workshop dates, Shon Feder opened a dune release announcement, and Tibor reported and attempted a cookbook link fix.
repositories.contributors:
- [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247) corrected the OCaml Workshop 2026 deadline dates in two successive PRs, first updating and then further refining the submission and notification dates shown on the workshop page ([#3707](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3707), [#3708](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3708))
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) opened a PR announcing the dune 3.24 release, requesting review from [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) ([#3704](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3704))
- [Tibor](https://github.com/Geerayef) reported a broken link in the Dream web-server cookbook and attempted to submit a fix, documenting a local opam/dune build failure encountered while setting up the dev environment ([#3703](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3703))

repositories.brief_summary: Dune 3.24.0 and a coordinated MirageOS release round shipped alongside several new package submissions.
repositories.new_features_summary: New packages (hegel, chatoyant, soteria, ocgtk, opam-minver) and releases of dune, js_of_ocaml, tls/x509, mirage, and lwt.

repositories.activity_summary: Several new releases (Caqti, Frama-C, caisar, colibri2, par/par_cli) remain open, and dune 3.24.0 prompted downstream fix-up PRs.
repositories.activity:
- Several PRs opened this week remain unmerged, including an **lmdb** 1.1.2 update by [@madroach](https://github.com/madroach) ([#30198](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30198)), a **caisar** 6.0 release by [caisar-platform](https://github.com/caisar-platform) ([#30165](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30165)), a **Frama-C** 33.0~beta-Arsenic release by [Allan Blanchard](https://github.com/AllanBlanchard) ([#30136](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30136)), a **Caqti** 3.0.0 release by [Petter A. Urkedal](https://github.com/paurkedal) ([#30174](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30174)), a **colibri2** 0.6 release (4 packages) by [François Bobot](https://github.com/bobot) ([#30097](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30097)), an **ocsigen-toolkit** 4.3.0 update by [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) ([#30181](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30181)), a checksum fix for the old **simple_httpd** 1.0 by [Christophe Raffalli](https://github.com/craff) ([#30123](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30123)), the new **par**/**par_cli** "Programmable Agent Runtime" packages by [JC](https://github.com/jcz2020) ([#30086](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30086)), and an **ocaml-r** 0.7.0 release by [pveber](https://github.com/pveber) ([#30007](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30007))
- Several submissions were closed without merging as contributors iterated toward a final version, including an **ocamlgrep** 0.1.0 package by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) ([#30200](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30200)), an **smtml** 0.29.0 package attempt by [@filipeom](https://github.com/filipeom) ([#30188](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30188)), and earlier chatoyant, hegel-ocaml, and opam-minver iterations superseded by their later merged versions ([#30180](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30180), [#30177](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30177), [#30170](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30170), [#30168](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30168), [#30164](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30164), [#30158](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30158), [#30152](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30152))

- The **dune** 3.24.0 release ([#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)) prompted a round of follow-up maintenance PRs bounding or disabling brittle downstream tests ([#30157](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30157), [#30162](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30162), [#30161](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30161), [#30160](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30160), [#30187](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30187), [#30183](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30183))
repositories.discussions_summary: Debate over who fixes dune-3.24-triggered revdep breakage, a simple_httpd checksum dispute, and OCaml 5.5.0 release-readiness tracking.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- Contributors debated ownership of revdep breakage following the dune 3.24.0 release, with [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) asking whether fixing packages broken by a dune release falls to the opam-repository maintainers, the dune maintainers, or the affected package's own maintainer ([#30157](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30157))
- [Christophe Raffalli](https://github.com/craff) and [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) agreed to mark the old **simple_httpd** 1.0 as unavailable rather than resolve a disputed checksum, after [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu) raised the discrepancy ([#30123](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30123))
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) tracked OCaml 5.5.0 release readiness ([#29463](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29463), closed), while a related bug where a second switch build of 5.5.0~alpha fails on macOS was reported by [@jrfondren](https://github.com/jrfondren) and traced to an upstream fix opened by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([#29496](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29496))
- [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) asked the community to stop running `dune runtest` unconditionally in CI, with [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu) noting it breaks tests tied to packages pending OCaml 5.5 support and [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) pointing to an existing mechanism for running a subset of tests ([#29166](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29166))
repositories.trends_summary: Coordinated MirageOS releases, OCaml 5.5.0 release prep, and dune-3.24 revdep fallout dominated the week.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) pushed a coordinated round of MirageOS-ecosystem releases spanning x509, tls, charrua, mirage, and kdf within the same week ([#30154](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30154), [#30155](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30155), [#30156](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30156), [#30184](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30184), [#30195](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30195), [#30166](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30166))
- The dune 3.24.0 release ([#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)) triggered a wave of downstream compatibility PRs bounding dune versions or disabling brittle tests, surfacing an open question about who owns fixing revdep breakage ([#30157](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30157), [#30162](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30162), [#30161](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30161), [#30160](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30160))
- Preparation for the OCaml 5.5.0 release continued, with a release-readiness tracking issue ([#29463](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29463)), a camlp5 release fixing a test broken by 5.5.0 ([#30163](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30163)), and the OCaml 5.4.0 manual package disabled over a bad checksum ([#30109](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30109))
- New package submissions touched on AI/agent tooling, including a "Programmable Agent Runtime" package ([#30086](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30086)) and a property-based testing library with built-in Antithesis support ([#30185](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30185))
