repositories.brief_summary: New CSS Font Loading, Service Worker/MessageChannel/Cache, and Web Worker toplevel bindings landed alongside a 6.4.1 dune-3.24 compatibility release.
repositories.new_features_summary: CSS Font Loading, Service Worker/MessageChannel/Cache API bindings, Web Worker toplevel, a tyxml attribute fix, and type-checked doc examples.
repositories.new_features:
- Partial **CSS Font Loading API** binding added via a new `FontFace` module (`fontFace`/`fontFaceSet` class types, `FontFace.create`) plus a `fonts` property on `Dom_html.document`, by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2255](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2255)), closing [#1833](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/1833) originally started by [Mesmera (WAFT)](https://github.com/mesmerizingwaft) and resolving [#66](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/66)-adjacent API-coverage requests
- New **Service Worker, MessageChannel, and Cache API** bindings by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2381](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2381)), including `serviceWorkerContainer`/`serviceWorkerRegistration`, the worker-side `serviceWorkerGlobalScope`, `MessageChannel`/`MessagePort`/`MessageEvent`, and `Cache`/`CacheStorage`, closing the long-standing feature request [#1464](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1464)
- **Toplevel `Wrapped` and `Async` APIs** plus a runnable **Web Worker toplevel** example landed by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2377](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2377)); `Wrapped` returns errors/warnings as first-class values instead of printing to stderr, and `Async` (new `js_of_ocaml-toplevel.lwt`-based sublibrary) drives the toplevel in a Web Worker via message passing — closing [#833](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/833) and addressing [#66](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/66)
- Fixed **repeated-attribute handling in Tyxml_js** (e.g. multiple `a_class`) so the first occurrence wins and only it is reactively subscribed, matching browser DOM parsing semantics instead of leaving multiple live signals fighting over the same attribute, by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2383](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2383)), fixing [#968](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/968) reported by [Clément Busschaert](https://github.com/clembu)
- Released **6.4.1**, a point release carrying only the dune-3.24 compatibility fixes (wat-module naming in `runtime/wasm/args.ml` and test path handling) on top of 6.4.0, by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2386](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2386), [#2384](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2384))
- Added **type-checked documentation examples**: `{@ocaml[ … ]}` code blocks in `.mli` doc-comments and `.mld` manual pages are now compiled against the real library under `dune build @runtest` (with `parse`/`skip`/`prelude` opt-out markers), by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2387](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2387)), closing [#1020](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1020) filed by [Olle Härstedt](https://github.com/olleharstedt); the checker caught and fixed several latent bugs in the manual and library doc-comments in the same PR
repositories.activity_summary: Five new PRs opened this week (WebGL2, MessageEvent consolidation, wasm identifier renaming, a compare-op fix, wasm asset resolution) alongside merged wasm runtime clean-up.
repositories.activity:
- Five **new PRs opened** this week and still under review: a draft **WebGL2 bindings** module by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) building on the existing WebGL binding ([#2388](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2388)), a draft consolidating five duplicate `messageEvent` class types into one shared `Dom_html.messageEvent` binding by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2390](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2390), fixing [#2382](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2382)), a rename of non-Rust-safe wasm `.wat` identifiers by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) ([#2392](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2392)), a fix restoring the immediate-vs-custom-block comparison path in `caml_compare_val` (needed for zarith-style custom blocks) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) ([#2391](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2391)), and a fix resolving wasm/WASI runtime assets relative to the runtime file instead of `require.main` by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) ([#2389](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2389))
- **Wasm runtime clean-up** merged by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) ([#2372](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2372)), touching 37 files as part of an ongoing migration of the `.wat` runtime sources toward the **Wax** authoring tool, with a further draft PR to switch runtime files to Wax outstanding ([#2373](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2373))
- Manual **sidebar ordering** for the Ocsigen documentation site continues as an open PR from [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat), adding `@children_order` so odoc lists manual pages in reading order rather than alphabetically ([#2359](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2359))
- A **compiler `subst` speedup** PR by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) was closed without merging ([#2185](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2185)), as was the draft **initial OCaml 5.5 support** PR touching 72 files ([#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2195)), and the superseded WIP toplevel interface ([#833](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/833)) and original CSS Font Loading attempt ([#1833](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/1833)) were both closed in favor of their successor PRs
- A long-pending PR to **consume compiler hints** produced by [ocaml/ocaml#13438](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/13438) was merged by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) after CI review ([#1721](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/1721))
- Several older **wasm-focused PRs** by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) remain open and were flagged by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) for a triage decision: more precise function/environment types ([#1908](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/1908)), more precise Wasm types generally ([#1907](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/1907)), an option to back OCaml strings with JavaScript strings ([#1772](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/1772)), and embedding Wasm code inside the JavaScript script ([#1758](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/1758))
repositories.discussions_summary: Reviewers discussed zarith custom-block comparison semantics and whether the new doc-example checker should move upstream into odoc.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- On the **compare-op regression fix** [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) raised open questions about why the zarith-related regression wasn't caught by existing tests, whether Jane Street tests should run under JS, and how the fix interacts with the wasm backend ([#2391](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2391))
- On the **type-checked doc examples** PR, [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) suggested the checking mechanism could eventually be upstreamed into `wodoc` or `odoc` itself, with [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) pointing to a related upstream discussion in [ocaml/odoc#303](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/pull/303) ([#2387](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2387))
- On the **wasm asset resolution fix**, [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) suggested relocating the fix's test to `tests-wasm_of_ocaml` since it only exercises wasm-specific behavior ([#2389](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2389))
repositories.trends_summary: Continued expansion of browser API bindings and an ongoing migration of the wasm runtime sources to the Wax authoring tool.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Steady **expansion of Web Platform API coverage**: CSS Font Loading ([#2255](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2255)) and Service Worker/MessageChannel/Cache ([#2381](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2381)) landed this week, with WebGL2 ([#2388](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2388)) and a MessageEvent-type consolidation ([#2390](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2390)) already in draft
- Ongoing migration of the **wasm runtime `.wat` sources** toward the Wax authoring tool, visible in the identifier-renaming work needed for Rust-compatible names ([#2392](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2392)), the runtime clean-up ([#2372](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2372)), and the outstanding switch-to-Wax draft ([#2373](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2373))
- Investment in **documentation reliability tooling**, with the new type-checked example-block system ([#2387](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2387)) immediately surfacing and fixing several latent doc-comment bugs across the manual and library interfaces
repositories.contributors_summary: Two contributors were active in code this week, alongside issue reporters and reviewers who shaped the merged work.

repositories.brief_summary: Added `array#`/`iarray#` unboxed types and external pointer primitives, fixed a memory corruption bug in ephemerons, and bumped the base to OCaml 5.4
repositories.new_features_summary: New unboxed `array#`/`iarray#` types, external pointer primitives, ELF notes for magic-trace, and several runtime/type-checker correctness fixes
repositories.new_features:
- Added unboxed **`array#` and `iarray#`** types of kind `any`, a step towards using boxed-index-free array representations, by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([#6361](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6361))
- Added **external pointer primitives** `%unsafe_set_ext_ptr`, `%unsafe_get_ext_ptr`, and `%unsafe_get_ext_ptr_imm` for libraries building off-heap single-word pointers without base+offset arithmetic, by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([#6350](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6350))
- Extended **scannable axis** support to arbitrary and abstract kinds and fixed/enabled the associated warnings, by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([#6367](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6367), [#6360](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6360), [#5874](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5874))
- Compiler hint reporting now emits **printed warnings instead of fatal errors**, avoiding hard failures for non-critical diagnostics, by [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) ([#6275](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6275))
- Added an all-void variant constructor **attribute requirement** to catch a class of unboxed-type mistakes at compile time, by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([#6256](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6256))
- Merged the **redundant modality warning** onto main, flagging module-level mode/modality annotations that are already implied, by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) ([#5532](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5532))
- Fixed a **hard-to-trigger memory corruption bug in ephemerons** by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) ([#6323](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6323)), plus related minor-GC correctness fixes for atomic release-store forwarding pointers ([#6330](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6330)) and continuation callstack tagging ([#6327](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6327))
- Fixed an **unaligned 128-bit load/store address computation** on arm64 by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) ([#6388](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6388)), and ensured the `Poll` CFG operation carries empty `arg`/`res` by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) ([#6326](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6326))
- Fixed **O(N²) data structures in the linker's module tracking**, improving link-time scaling for large programs, by [Nicolas Trangez](https://github.com/NicolasT) ([#5807](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5807))
- Marked additional flambda2 functions `[@inline]` to reduce **compilation time**, by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) ([#6376](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6376))
- Added an `ocaml_eff` **ELF notes section** recording effect-implementation branch offsets, replacing hard-coded offsets previously needed by the `magic-trace` tool, by [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) ([#6274](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6274))

repositories.activity_summary: 27 PRs opened this week remain under review, including a Merlin type-recovery integration and AVX512 intrinsics, alongside ongoing SSA-backend and fiber-dynamics work
repositories.activity:

repositories.discussions_summary: Extended review threads on the SSA backend, fiber dynamic-binding tables, and dynamic staticity PRs surfaced design disagreements over comment style and layout
repositories.notable_discussions:
- Review of [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet)'s SSA intermediate representation ([#5810](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5810)) drew close scrutiny from [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) over apparent regressions and warning-suppression choices, spanning nearly 40 review comments
- [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark)'s review of the fiber dynamic-binding table redesign ([#6359](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6359)) pushed for clearer invariant documentation, better grep-ability of stack-context word counts, and reconsideration of struct layout for `stack_info`
- Reviewers on [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn)'s dynamic-staticity PR ([#6226](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6226)) including [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) pushed back on AI-generated comment style in the code, calling it inconsistent with the codebase's conventions and often unhelpful
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) requested that the redundant-modifier warning ([#5436](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5436)) explain *why* a modifier is redundant (e.g. "because it is implied by global") rather than just flagging it, and questioned the non-uniform treatment across tests
- Reviewers on the `alloc_regions` plumbing PR ([#6121](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6121)) including [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) probed edge cases around ghost regions and requested expanded explanatory comments over inline `XXX` markers
repositories.trends_summary: Continued focus on unboxed-types/kind-system refinement, runtime correctness hardening by a single maintainer, and flambda2/reaper bottom-environment cleanup
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Continued incremental expansion of the **unboxed types and kind system**, with scannable axes, `array#`/`iarray#`, and all-void variant attribute requirements landing in the same week from [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([#6361](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6361), [#6367](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6367), [#6360](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6360), [#5874](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5874), [#6256](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6256))
- [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) is systematically hardening the **multicore runtime**, merging eight distinct fixes this week touching ephemerons, minor GC forwarding pointers, thread ticks, and fiber dynamic-binding caches ([#6323](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6323), [#6330](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6330), [#6327](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6327), [#6329](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6329), [#6331](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6331), [#6333](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6333), [#6341](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6341), [#6345](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6345))
- Flambda2/reaper work is converging on eliminating **"bottom" environments** during join and simplification, with three related merges from [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) ([#6348](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6348), [#6349](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6349)) and continued discussion in his open BFS meet-strategy PR ([#6191](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6191))

repositories.brief_summary: A macOS build failure in `posixat` was reported as an issue against the OxCaml opam switch.
repositories.issues_summary: A build-compatibility issue on macOS for the `posixat` package was opened, describing missing Linux-specific headers.
repositories.good_first_issues: - [Sacha Ayoun](https://github.com/giltho) opened an issue describing `posixat` failing to compile on macOS/arm64 when installed via an OxCaml switch, due to missing `linux/openat2.h` and an undeclared `renameat2` identifier used by the generated stubs ([#53](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/53))
repositories.contributors_summary: Sacha Ayoun reported a macOS build failure affecting the `posixat` package.
repositories.contributors: - [Sacha Ayoun](https://github.com/giltho) filed a bug report describing a compilation failure of `posixat` on macOS/arm64 when installed through an OxCaml switch, including full build logs showing the missing `linux/openat2.h` header and undeclared `renameat2` symbol ([#53](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/53))

repositories.brief_summary: Release v2.15.0 shipped with per-operation RWF flags and a liburing 2.15 update, plus fixes for opam sandbox builds and Eio compatibility.
repositories.new_features_summary: Per-operation RWF flags for read/write ops and vendored liburing bumped to 2.15, released as v2.15.0.
repositories.new_features:
- New `Rw_flags` module exposes per-operation `preadv2`/`pwritev2` flags (`hipri`, `dsync`, `sync`, `nowait`, `append`, `noappend`, `atomic`, `dontcache`) and threads an optional `?flags` argument through `read`, `write`, `readv`, `writev`, `read_fixed`, `read_chunk`, `write_fixed` and `write_chunk`, letting callers request per-write durability (`dsync`/`sync`) or drop pages from the cache after streaming I/O (`dontcache`) without a separate `fsync` call, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154))
- Vendored `liburing` updated to 2.15, pulling in a new `bpf_filter.h` header and the corresponding `liburing.so.2.15` shared library into the `dune` build rules, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157))
- `FLAGS.t` restored to `private int` (rather than made fully abstract) so that existing code depending on the underlying integer representation, such as Eio, keeps working ([ocaml-multicore/eio#785](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/785)), by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#159](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/159))
- Fixed the vendored `configure` test to avoid writing to `/tmp`, which opam's build sandbox marks read-only and had broken installation of `uring.dev`, by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#158](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/158))
- Release [v2.15.0](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/releases/tag/v2.15.0) published, bundling this week's `Rw_flags` and liburing 2.15 work together with earlier accumulated new ops and bug fixes, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)
- `README.md` subtree-pull instructions updated to reference the `liburing-2.15` tag, and `tests/main.md` gained a worked example showing `dsync` writes, flag-free `readv`, and the `EOPNOTSUPP` fallback path when an unsupported flag bit is set, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154), [#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157))
repositories.activity_summary: Three PRs opened and merged within the week for build sandboxing and FLAGS.t compatibility, alongside the RWF flags work and the v2.15.0 release.
repositories.activity:
- All three PRs opened this week were merged the same week: the liburing 2.15 update by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157)), followed in quick succession by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5)'s opam-sandbox build fix ([#158](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/158)) and `FLAGS.t` compatibility revert ([#159](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/159)), both merged within a day of opening.
- The per-operation `RWF` flags PR opened in the prior week by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) was merged this week ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154)), rounding out the API work that fed into the [v2.15.0](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/releases/tag/v2.15.0) release published by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm).
- Discussion continued on the open issue about building without vendored `liburing`, with [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) explaining the opam-distribution rationale and exploring an optional environment-variable toggle for a future version ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156)).
repositories.discussions_summary: Ongoing debate on issue #156 over vendoring liburing versus depending on a system-provided version.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) argued against vendoring `liburing` and asked for a way to build against the system library, citing a Debian 13 packaging mismatch on the minimum required version; [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) responded that vendoring is important for opam distribution while `liburing` remains unavailable on several distros, noted he explored an optional environment-variable toggle to decouple it but found it messy, and agreed with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5)'s suggestion that a packaging-layer patch is the best near-term fix ([#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156)).
- Review on the liburing 2.15 update surfaced an opam sandbox build failure (`mktemp` failing against a read-only `/tmp`) caught by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) shortly after merge, resolved via a follow-up fix that was folded into the same buildable history ([#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157), [#158](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/158)).
repositories.trends_summary: Continued emphasis on Eio compatibility and opam sandbox build robustness while adopting newer liburing/io_uring capabilities.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Care to preserve downstream Eio compatibility when evolving the API surface, as seen in the `FLAGS.t` revert to keep the type's integer representation available ([#159](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/159), [ocaml-multicore/eio#785](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/785)).
- Attention to opam's sandboxed build environment, with a second `/tmp`-related build fix landing this week after the liburing 2.15 vendor update exposed the issue ([#158](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/158)).
- Incremental exposure of newer Linux `io_uring`/`liburing` capabilities at the OCaml layer, from per-operation `RWF` flags to the BPF filter header pulled in with liburing 2.15 ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154), [#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157)).
repositories.contributors_summary: Anil Madhavapeddy shipped RWF flags, liburing 2.15, and the release; Thomas Leonard fixed a build bug; Stéphane Glondu raised vendoring concerns.
repositories.contributors:
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) implemented the `Rw_flags` API for per-operation read/write flags ([#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154)), updated the vendored `liburing` to 2.15 including the new BPF filter header ([#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157)), published the [v2.15.0](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/releases/tag/v2.15.0) release, and responded to [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu)'s vendoring concerns on issue [#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156).
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) reviewed the liburing 2.15 update and caught an opam sandbox build failure, fixing the vendored `configure` test's use of `/tmp` ([#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/157), [#158](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/158)), restored `FLAGS.t`'s private-int representation to avoid breaking Eio ([#159](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/159)), and commented on the vendoring discussion in [#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156).
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) opened the discussion on issue [#156](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/156) requesting a way to build without vendored `liburing`, explaining the Debian packaging constraint that motivated the request.

repositories.brief_summary: Eio gained file-ownership (`chown`) and pseudoterminal (`Pty`) support, plus a Windows fd-tracking fix and a `Pool.use` signature change.
repositories.new_features_summary: New `Path.chown`/`Eio_unix.Pty` APIs, a `login_tty` fork action, a Windows poll-set fix, and a `Pool.use` argument reorder.
repositories.new_features:
- Added `Eio.Path.chown` for changing file/symlink ownership, implemented across the `posix`, `linux` and `windows` backends, by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) with follow-up flag fixes from [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781), [#864](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/864))
- Introduced `Eio_unix.Pty` for creating and controlling **pseudoterminals**, including window-size get/set and a `Tc` module for termios attributes (`getattr`/`setattr`/`drain`/`flush`/`flow`), plus a `Fork_action.login_tty` action so a child process can be given a pty as its controlling terminal, by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/531))
- Fixed an **`eio_windows`** scheduler bug where a file descriptor being watched for both read and write, then switched to only one, was left registered in the other poll set, causing the high-CPU busy-loop reported in [#837](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/837); fixed by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#861](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/861))
- Reordered the optional `?never_block` argument in `Eio.Pool.use` to come before the pool argument, a **breaking API change** that makes it obvious to the compiler when the argument is unset, by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#747](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/747))
- Raised the minimum `uring` dependency to `2.15.0+` to unlock newer functionality, landed directly on `main` by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([8b7b4ed](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/commit/8b7b4edebc359d98f9512f4045b8f652985c4b77), tracked in [#867](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/867))
- Added ownership test coverage to `tests/fs.md` covering `chown` on files, hard links and symlinks with `~follow`, and a new `tests/pty.md` walking through pty creation, window sizing, termios attributes and spawning a child with a pty as its controlling terminal ([#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781), [#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/531))
repositories.activity_summary: Two PRs opened this week remain unmerged (uring bump, statx-based fstat), while chown, pty and Windows-poll work landed.
repositories.activity:
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) opened [#867](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/867) to bump the minimum `uring` version to `2.15.0+` and [#865](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/865) to switch Linux `fstat` to use `statx` so that `blksize`/`blocks` are populated for disk-usage calculations, both still open at week's end
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) opened [#866](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/866) so that `Net.getaddrinfo` reports the underlying resolver error instead of silently returning an empty list, fixing the too-many-open-files case from [#776](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/776) and adding `AI_ADDRCONFIG` behaviour on the `linux`/`posix` backends, under review from [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)
- The `chown` follow/nofollow flag bug was fixed twice in parallel: [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) opened [#863](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/863) correcting the `AT_SYMLINK_FOLLOW`/`AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW` mixup spotted by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) in [#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781), then closed it in favour of [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)'s smaller fix in [#864](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/864), which also added Linux and cross-platform ownership tests
- The long-running **pseudoterminal** effort ([#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/531)), open since 2023-05-28, was merged this week after review from [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) and [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris), fixing OpenBSD build issues and FD-leak edge cases along the way
- The Windows high-CPU regression reported in [#837](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/837) by [张睿 Zhang Rui](https://github.com/z-rui) for a bidirectional TCP proxy was fixed and closed via [#861](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/861)
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion continued on unexplained io_uring IO-pressure reporting and a proposal for standard temp-directory helpers.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Marijan Petričević](https://github.com/marijanp) reported persistently high `/proc/pressure/io` readings for an otherwise idle `io_uring`-backed service in [#862](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/862); [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) pointed to background reading on how the kernel accounts for `iowait` with `io_uring`, and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) suggested comparing against the `posix` backend to isolate the cause
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) proposed adding `Stdenv.tmp` and `Path.with_temp_dir`/`open_temp_dir` helpers for managing temporary directories in [#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831); [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) agreed something is needed but suggested it could live directly under `Eio_unix` rather than `Stdenv`, since it doesn't need the same access-restriction treatment as other capabilities
repositories.trends_summary: Filesystem-metadata coverage (chown, statx) and terminal/process control are both expanding, alongside continued Windows-backend hardening.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Filesystem metadata APIs are being filled out to match OCaml's `Unix` module, with `chown` landing this week ([#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781), [#864](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/864)) and a `statx`-based `fstat` for `blksize`/`blocks` under review ([#865](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/865)), tracked against the broader missing-operations list in [#510](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/510)
- Process and terminal control is deepening with the new `Pty` module and `login_tty` fork action ([#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/531)), extending beyond plain process spawning
- The Windows backend continues to receive targeted reliability fixes for its `select`-based scheduler, following the read/write poll-set bug fix in [#861](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/861)
repositories.contributors_summary: Core maintainers avsm and talex5 drove chown, pty and Windows-poll work, with community reports from marijanp, z-rui and vog shaping the queue.
repositories.contributors:
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) authored and merged the pseudoterminal/`login_tty` support ([#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/531)), fixed the `chown` symlink-flag bug and added cross-platform tests ([#864](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/864)), opened the `uring` minimum-version bump and the `statx`-based `fstat` change ([#867](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/867), [#865](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/865)), and reviewed [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris)'s `chown` PR and [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5)'s `getaddrinfo` error-reporting PR, and commented on the `io_uring` IO-pressure report ([#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781), [#866](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/866), [#862](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/862))
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) merged the `chown` support and rebased/adjusted it for optional `uid`/`gid` ([#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781)), reviewed and merged the pty work ([#531](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/531)), fixed the Windows read/write poll-set bug ([#861](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/861)), reordered `Pool.use`'s optional argument ([#747](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/747)), opened and then closed a `chown` flag fix in favour of a smaller alternative ([#863](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/863)), opened the `getaddrinfo` error-reporting PR ([#866](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/866)), and discussed `io_uring` IO-pressure accounting and the temp-directory proposal ([#862](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/862), [#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831))
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) authored the original `chown` implementation for `eio_posix` and `eio_linux` ([#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781))
- [@marijanp](https://github.com/marijanp) filed a report of high `io_uring`-related IO pressure on an idle service ([#862](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/862))
- [@z-rui](https://github.com/z-rui) reported the Windows high-CPU two-way-copy bug with a minimal reproduction, which was fixed this week ([#837](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/837))
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) proposed temporary-directory helper APIs ([#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831))

group_summaries.brief_summary: Dune 3.24.0 shipped in opam-repository and was announced on ocaml.org, alongside coordinated MirageOS releases and new packages.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Dune 3.24.0 and coordinated MirageOS releases shipped in opam-repository; ocaml.org corrected its OCaml Workshop 2026 dates.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Dune 3.24.0** released (18 packages) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) in opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)), with a companion announcement PR opened on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3704](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3704))
- Coordinated **MirageOS ecosystem** releases by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) covering x509 1.1.1, tls 2.1.1, charrua 3.1.2/3.2.0, mirage 4.11.1, and kdf 1.1.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#30154](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30154), [ocaml/opam-repository#30155](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30155), [ocaml/opam-repository#30156](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30156))
- New **hegel** property-based testing library with Antithesis support, plus PPX helpers, submitted by [Ethan Chou](https://github.com/echoumcp1) after several iterations ([ocaml/opam-repository#30185](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30185))
- New **ocgtk** GTK4 bindings and **conf-gtk4** dependency by [Chris Armstrong](https://github.com/chris-armstrong) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30134](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30134))
- **js_of_ocaml**/**wasm_of_ocaml** 6.4.1 release (8 packages) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30173](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30173))
- Around a dozen further new packages submitted, including chatoyant, soteria, playwright, menhirformat, keyseq, melange-edn, starpath, and opam-minver ([ocaml/opam-repository#30190](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30190), [ocaml/opam-repository#30150](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30150), [ocaml/opam-repository#30146](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30146))
- Fixed the **OCaml Workshop 2026 "Important Dates" panel** on ocaml.org, correcting the submission deadline to 12 Jul and the notification date to 3 Aug by [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3707](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3707), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3708](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3708))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **ocaml/opam-repository**: dune 3.24.0 (18 packages) and a coordinated MirageOS release round (x509, tls, charrua, mirage, kdf) shipped alongside roughly 15 new package submissions ([ocaml/opam-repository#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092), [ocaml/opam-repository#30154](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30154))
- **ocaml/ocaml.org**: corrected OCaml Workshop 2026 deadline dates, continued automated Planet-blog and platform-release ingestion, and had a broken cookbook link reported ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3707](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3707), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3703](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3703))
- Dune's 3.24.0 release ([ocaml/opam-repository#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)) was the week's shared thread, prompting both downstream package fixes in opam-repository and a pending announcement post on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3704](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3704))
group_summaries.activity_summary: Several opam-repository release PRs remain open, and ocaml.org's Planet-scrape ingestion continued alongside a reported broken cookbook link.
group_summaries.activity:
- Several opam-repository release PRs remain unmerged, including lmdb 1.1.2, caisar 6.0, Frama-C 33.0~beta-Arsenic, Caqti 3.0.0, colibri2 0.6, and ocsigen-toolkit 4.3.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#30198](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30198), [ocaml/opam-repository#30136](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30136), [ocaml/opam-repository#30174](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30174))
- The dune 3.24.0 release prompted a wave of downstream maintenance PRs in opam-repository bounding dune versions or disabling brittle tests ([ocaml/opam-repository#30157](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30157), [ocaml/opam-repository#30162](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30162), [ocaml/opam-repository#30161](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30161))
- On ocaml.org, three Planet scrape PRs merged bringing in new blog posts and an OCaml Weekly News edition, while an automated platform-releases scrape PR (212 lines, four files) remains open from before this week ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3702](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3702), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3705](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3705), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3693](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3693))
- A broken link in the ocaml.org Dream web-server cookbook was reported, pointing to a stale `aantron.github.io/dream` URL needing redirection to `camlworks.github.io/dream` ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3703](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3703))
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- Dune 3.24.0 shipped in opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), who also opened the corresponding release-announcement PR on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3704](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3704)), still awaiting review from [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) at week's end
- The dune 3.24.0 release's downstream compatibility fallout (bounding versions, disabling brittle tests) was handled entirely within opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#30157](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30157), [ocaml/opam-repository#30162](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30162)), while ocaml.org's coverage stayed at the announcement stage, pending merge
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) was active across the group this week, releasing uring 2.15.0 in opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#30196](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30196)) while a personal blog post was separately ingested into ocaml.org's Planet feed via automated scraping ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3702](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3702))
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- Contributors debated ownership of revdep breakage following the dune 3.24.0 release, with [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) asking whether fixing packages broken by a dune release falls to opam-repository maintainers, dune maintainers, or the affected package's own maintainer ([ocaml/opam-repository#30157](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30157))
- [Christophe Raffalli](https://github.com/craff) and [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) agreed to mark the old **simple_httpd** 1.0 as unavailable rather than resolve a disputed checksum ([ocaml/opam-repository#30123](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30123))
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) tracked OCaml 5.5.0 release readiness ([ocaml/opam-repository#29463](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29463)), while a related macOS second-switch build failure for 5.5.0~alpha was reported by [@jrfondren](https://github.com/jrfondren) and traced to an upstream fix by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29496](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29496))
- [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) asked the community to stop running `dune runtest` unconditionally in CI, with [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu) and [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) discussing existing mechanisms for running a subset of tests ([ocaml/opam-repository#29166](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29166))

group_summaries.emerging_trends:

- The dune 3.24.0 release ([ocaml/opam-repository#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)) triggered downstream compatibility fixes in opam-repository and surfaced an open question about who owns fixing revdep breakage ([ocaml/opam-repository#30157](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30157))

- ocaml.org content this week was driven mainly by recurring automated ingestion (Planet blog scraping, platform release tracking) rather than new manual authoring ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3702](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3702), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3693](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3693))

group_summaries.brief_summary: A typechecking speedup and an ephemeron GC fix merged in ocaml/ocaml, while ocaml/RFCs received a new proposal for a destinations API.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Typechecking speedup (ocaml/ocaml#14900), ephemeron GC marking fix (ocaml/ocaml#14688), and a ctype scope bug fix (ocaml/ocaml#14892) merged.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Typechecking performance** improved by removing closure allocations from `deep_occur_rec`, which traverses type expressions during unification; benchmarks on a large `js_of_ocaml` file show up to a 45% wall-clock speedup with flambda and 20% without, by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) ([ocaml/ocaml#14900](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14900)), motivated by the regression reported in [ocaml/ocaml#14897](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14897)
- **Major GC ephemeron marking** reworked so `ephe_mark()` calls `mark()` directly on markable data instead of only scheduling it, avoiding quadratic-looking marking behaviour for long ephemeron dependency chains, by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) ([ocaml/ocaml#14688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14688)), a safer follow-up to the previously reverted [ocaml/ocaml#14657](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14657)
- Fixed a **scope error in `ctype`** where non-dependent module-type application could erase the whole result type after the first package type was encountered, causing incorrect types to be accepted for anonymous module type structs, by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([ocaml/ocaml#14892](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14892), fixing [ocaml/ocaml#14891](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14891))
- Removed a useless `Option.map` call in `Btype.fold_row`, a small internal cleanup noticed while working on the typechecking performance PR, by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) ([ocaml/ocaml#14904](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14904))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) merged a typechecking performance fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14900](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14900), up to 45% wall-clock speedup on large types) and an ephemeron GC marking fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14688))
- A `ctype` scope-error fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14892](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14892)) and a small internal cleanup ([ocaml/ocaml#14904](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14904)) also landed in ocaml/ocaml
- New PRs were opened in ocaml/ocaml for stdlib additions ([ocaml/ocaml#14909](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14909)), a `caml_shutdown`/ephemeron termination bug ([ocaml/ocaml#14906](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14906)), large-interval pattern matching ([ocaml/ocaml#14907](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14907)), and an `opam clone` macOS fix targeted for 5.5.1 ([ocaml/ocaml#14901](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14901))
- Design discussion continued on a static-linking / link-time parameter resolution proposal in ocaml/ocaml ([ocaml/ocaml#14895](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14895)), with reviewers requesting a formal design document
- [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) received one new proposal, [ocaml/RFCs#62](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/62), for a compiler-assisted API exposing destinations (write-once pointers) to PPXs, opened by [Gabriel Radanne](https://github.com/Drup)
group_summaries.activity_summary: New ocaml/ocaml PRs for stdlib additions, pattern matching, opam macOS support, and a caml_shutdown fix; a new ocaml/RFCs proposal on destinations.
group_summaries.activity:
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) opened **`Dynarray.append_iarray` and `Pqueue.of_iarray`** stdlib additions to avoid copying an `iarray` into a mutable array or resorting to unsafe code ([ocaml/ocaml#14909](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14909), fixing [ocaml/ocaml#14320](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14320))
- [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) opened a fix for **`caml_shutdown()` termination with ephemerons**, addressing an infinite loop in `Phase_sweep_and_mark_main` when the last domain is terminated with `OCAMLRUNPARAM=c=1`, joint work with [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) that has been affecting CI for weeks ([ocaml/ocaml#14906](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14906), related to [ocaml/ocaml#14594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14594))
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened a **pattern-matching compiler** change to support switches over very large integer intervals (beyond the previous $[-2^{16}; 2^{16}]$ limit), with [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) and [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) reviewing edge cases around interval overflow ([ocaml/ocaml#14907](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14907))
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened a fix for **`opam clone` on macOS**, where the default `/bin/sh` (old bash) mishandles heredocs and creates temporary files in the wrong directory, targeted for the 5.5.1 release per [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) ([ocaml/ocaml#14901](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14901), fixing [ocaml/opam-repository#29496](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29496))
- Ongoing design discussion on [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust)'s **static linking and link-time linking parameter resolution** proposal (`-cclib-static`, `-cclib-variant`, `-static`, `-linking-variant`), with [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) requesting a proper design document referencing his own OCaml library linking RFC and [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) noting relevance to MirageOS/Solo5 unikernel toolchains ([ocaml/ocaml#14895](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14895))
- [Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) continued iterating on the **"Signature mismatch" error message rewrite** (renaming "is not included"/"does not include" to subtype/supertype terminology), with extensive wording feedback from [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) and [Brandon Stride](https://github.com/brandonzstride) ([ocaml/ocaml#14896](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14896), following discussion in [ocaml/ocaml#11937](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11937))
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened a **guideline for LLM usage in bug reports**, recommending against mixing LLM-generated content with bug reports and cautioning about LLM-shrunk reproduction cases losing the original bug, reviewed by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole), [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli), [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), and [Miod Vallat](https://github.com/miodvallat) ([ocaml/ocaml#14899](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14899))
- [Anarchos](https://github.com/Sylvain78) continued the **Haiku port** PR covering unix socket tests, single-threaded `setlocale`, and signal handling differences on Haiku, still marked as conflicting ([ocaml/ocaml#14773](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14773))
- [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist)'s PR on **missing minor-heap allocation statistics for the main domain at exit** remains under review, with [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) proposing alternatives to the extra minor-GC-at-exit approach ([ocaml/ocaml#14522](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14522))
- [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist)'s **s390x CFI/backtrace fix**, merged earlier this year, was confirmed and its tracking issue closed this week ([ocaml/ocaml#14500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14500), closing [ocaml/ocaml#13509](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13509))
- [Gabriel Radanne](https://github.com/Drup) opened a new **RFC** proposing to expose destinations (write-once pointers used in tail-modulo-cons and related destination-passing-style transformations) so they can be manipulated from PPXs rather than only from the compiler's internal Lambda IR ([ocaml/RFCs#62](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/62)); the proposal remains open and awaiting review, with no comments yet recorded
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- [ocaml/RFCs#62](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/62) proposes exposing destinations (write-once pointers) via a compiler-assisted API; if accepted, this would require corresponding implementation work in ocaml/ocaml's Lambda IR and typedtree, alongside the destination-passing-style transformations (such as tail-modulo-cons) already implemented internally in the ocaml/ocaml compiler
- The request for a formal design document on the static-linking proposal in [ocaml/ocaml#14895](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14895) mirrors the design-review process that the ocaml/RFCs repository formalizes, though the specific linking RFC referenced by [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) is hosted outside ocaml/RFCs itself
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- Extensive back-and-forth on rewording the **"Signature mismatch" compiler error message**, weighing "subtype"/"supertype" terminology against readability and consistency with existing messages about `include`, involving [Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse), [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), and [Brandon Stride](https://github.com/brandonzstride) ([ocaml/ocaml#14896](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14896))
- [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) pushed back on the **static/link-time linking proposal**, arguing that adding new `.cma` metadata fields and a shell-command-based `pkg-config` resolution mechanism needs a design document before merging, citing past problems with ad-hoc `META` file metadata and raising a security concern about archives embedding shell commands run at link time; [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) offered a MirageOS/Solo5 use case as a concrete test ([ocaml/ocaml#14895](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14895))
- Deep investigation into the **typing performance regression** in `js_of_ocaml`'s `lwt_xmlHttpRequest.ml`, with [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue), and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) diagnosing that unification of deeply nested object types causes repeated, non-shared expansion of type abbreviations ([ocaml/ocaml#14897](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14897), related to [ocaml/ocaml#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648) and [ocaml/ocaml#14801](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14801))
- [Simmo Saan](https://github.com/sim642) shared updated **Goblint benchmark results** comparing OCaml 4.14 against 5.6.0+trunk (including [ocaml/ocaml#14885](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14885) and [ocaml/ocaml#14894](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14894)), showing average CPU time regression down to +4% from earlier measurements, prompting [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) to ask about remaining outlier tests ([ocaml/ocaml#13733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13733))
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- A cluster of **typechecking and GC performance** work landed or progressed in ocaml/ocaml this week, including `deep_occur_rec` allocation removal ([ocaml/ocaml#14900](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14900)), ephemeron marking ([ocaml/ocaml#14688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14688)), and ongoing Goblint/Frama-C regression tracking ([ocaml/ocaml#13733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13733), [ocaml/ocaml#11733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11733)), with [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) also reporting `-O3` and inlining-attribute experiments that cut `deep_occur_rec` time further
- Maintainers are formalizing **process around AI-assisted contributions** to ocaml/ocaml, with [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)'s bug-report guideline proposal ([ocaml/ocaml#14899](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14899)) following earlier discussion in [ocaml/ocaml#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842)
- Recurring pattern of **closure-allocation elimination in the typechecker** (renamings in recursive definitions from [ocaml/ocaml#14885](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14885)/[ocaml/ocaml#14894](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14894), and `deep_occur_rec` from [ocaml/ocaml#14900](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14900)) as a recurring technique for compile-time speedups, motivated by real-world regressions reported by users of `js_of_ocaml` and Goblint
- The single ocaml/RFCs proposal this week ([ocaml/RFCs#62](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/62)) continues a pattern of exposing compiler-internal mechanisms (here, destinations) to library and PPX authors rather than keeping them restricted to the compiler's internal IR

weekly_summary.activity_summary: New ocaml/ocaml PRs for stdlib, pattern matching and opam macOS fixes; opam-repository saw dune-driven downstream fixes and new packages.
weekly_summary.activity:
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) opened **`Dynarray.append_iarray` and `Pqueue.of_iarray`** stdlib additions to avoid copying an `iarray` into a mutable array or resorting to unsafe code ([ocaml/ocaml#14909](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14909), fixing [ocaml/ocaml#14320](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14320))
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) opened a fix for **`caml_shutdown()` termination with ephemerons**, addressing an infinite loop in `Phase_sweep_and_mark_main` when the last domain is terminated with `OCAMLRUNPARAM=c=1`, joint work with [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) that has been affecting CI for weeks ([ocaml/ocaml#14906](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14906), related to [ocaml/ocaml#14594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14594))
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened a **pattern-matching compiler** change to support switches over very large integer intervals (beyond the previous $[-2^{16}; 2^{16}]$ limit), with [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) and [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) reviewing edge cases around interval overflow ([ocaml/ocaml#14907](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14907))
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened a fix for **`opam clone` on macOS**, where the default `/bin/sh` (old bash) mishandles heredocs and creates temporary files in the wrong directory, targeted for the 5.5.1 release per [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) ([ocaml/ocaml#14901](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14901), fixing [ocaml/opam-repository#29496](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29496))
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) continued iterating on the **"Signature mismatch" error message rewrite** (renaming "is not included"/"does not include" to subtype/supertype terminology), with extensive wording feedback from [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) and [Brandon Stride](https://github.com/brandonzstride) ([ocaml/ocaml#14896](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14896), following discussion in [ocaml/ocaml#11937](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11937))
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened a **guideline for LLM usage in bug reports**, recommending against mixing LLM-generated content with bug reports and cautioning about LLM-shrunk reproduction cases losing the original bug, reviewed by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole), [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli), [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), and [Miod Vallat](https://github.com/miodvallat) ([ocaml/ocaml#14899](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14899))
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Anarchos](https://github.com/Sylvain78) continued the **Haiku port** PR covering unix socket tests, single-threaded `setlocale`, and signal handling differences on Haiku, still marked as conflicting ([ocaml/ocaml#14773](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14773))
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist)'s PR on **missing minor-heap allocation statistics for the main domain at exit** remains under review, with [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) proposing alternatives to the extra minor-GC-at-exit approach ([ocaml/ocaml#14522](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14522)); his earlier **s390x CFI/backtrace fix** was confirmed and its tracking issue closed this week ([ocaml/ocaml#14500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14500), closing [ocaml/ocaml#13509](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13509))
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Gabriel Radanne](https://github.com/Drup) opened a new **RFC** proposing to expose destinations (write-once pointers used in tail-modulo-cons and related destination-passing-style transformations) so they can be manipulated from PPXs rather than only from the compiler's internal Lambda IR ([ocaml/RFCs#62](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/62)); the proposal remains open and awaiting review
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Several opam-repository release PRs remain unmerged, including lmdb 1.1.2, caisar 6.0, Frama-C 33.0~beta-Arsenic, Caqti 3.0.0, colibri2 0.6, and ocsigen-toolkit 4.3.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#30198](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30198), [ocaml/opam-repository#30136](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30136), [ocaml/opam-repository#30174](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30174))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ The dune 3.24.0 release prompted a wave of downstream maintenance PRs in opam-repository bounding dune versions or disabling brittle tests ([ocaml/opam-repository#30157](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30157), [ocaml/opam-repository#30162](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30162), [ocaml/opam-repository#30161](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30161))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ On ocaml.org, three Planet scrape PRs merged bringing in new blog posts and an OCaml Weekly News edition, while an automated platform-releases scrape PR (212 lines, four files) remains open from before this week ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3702](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3702), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3705](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3705), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3693](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3693))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ A broken link in the ocaml.org Dream web-server cookbook was reported, pointing to a stale `aantron.github.io/dream` URL needing redirection to `camlworks.github.io/dream` ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3703](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3703))

weekly_summary.cross_repository_work:
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Dune 3.24.0 shipped in opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), who also opened the corresponding release-announcement PR on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3704](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3704)), still awaiting review from [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) at week's end

- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ The dune 3.24.0 release's broader downstream compatibility fallout (bounding versions, disabling brittle tests) was handled within opam-repository across multiple packages ([ocaml/opam-repository#30157](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30157), [ocaml/opam-repository#30162](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30162))
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) linked the ocaml/ocaml **static linking / link-time parameter resolution** proposal ([ocaml/ocaml#14895](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14895)) to MirageOS/Solo5 unikernel toolchain needs, connecting compiler-level design work to the __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ MirageOS release round shipped the same week by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30154](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30154))
- __RUMINANT:core__ The typing-performance regression fixed in ocaml/ocaml ([ocaml/ocaml#14900](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14900)) was originally diagnosed against a real-world __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ `js_of_ocaml` source file ([ocaml/ocaml#14897](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14897)), tying compiler typechecker work directly to an ecosystem tool's build times

weekly_summary.notable_discussions:
- __RUMINANT:core__ Extensive back-and-forth on rewording the **"Signature mismatch" compiler error message**, weighing "subtype"/"supertype" terminology against readability and consistency with existing messages about `include`, involving [Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse), [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), and [Brandon Stride](https://github.com/brandonzstride) ([ocaml/ocaml#14896](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14896))
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) pushed back on the **static/link-time linking proposal**, arguing that adding new `.cma` metadata fields and a shell-command-based `pkg-config` resolution mechanism needs a design document before merging, citing past problems with ad-hoc `META` file metadata and raising a security concern about archives embedding shell commands run at link time; [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) offered a MirageOS/Solo5 use case as a concrete test ([ocaml/ocaml#14895](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14895))
- __RUMINANT:core__ Deep investigation into the **typing performance regression** in `js_of_ocaml`'s `lwt_xmlHttpRequest.ml`, with [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue), and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) diagnosing that unification of deeply nested object types causes repeated, non-shared expansion of type abbreviations ([ocaml/ocaml#14897](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14897), related to [ocaml/ocaml#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648) and [ocaml/ocaml#14801](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14801))
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Simmo Saan](https://github.com/sim642) shared updated **Goblint benchmark results** comparing OCaml 4.14 against 5.6.0+trunk (including [ocaml/ocaml#14885](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14885) and [ocaml/ocaml#14894](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14894)), showing average CPU time regression down to +4% from earlier measurements, prompting [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) to ask about remaining outlier tests ([ocaml/ocaml#13733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13733))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Contributors debated ownership of revdep breakage following the dune 3.24.0 release, with [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) asking whether fixing packages broken by a dune release falls to opam-repository maintainers, dune maintainers, or the affected package's own maintainer ([ocaml/opam-repository#30157](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30157))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [Christophe Raffalli](https://github.com/craff) and [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) agreed to mark the old **simple_httpd** 1.0 as unavailable rather than resolve a disputed checksum ([ocaml/opam-repository#30123](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30123))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) tracked OCaml 5.5.0 release readiness ([ocaml/opam-repository#29463](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29463)), while a related macOS second-switch build failure for 5.5.0~alpha was reported by [@jrfondren](https://github.com/jrfondren) and traced to an upstream fix by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29496](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29496))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) asked the community to stop running `dune runtest` unconditionally in CI, with [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu) and [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) discussing existing mechanisms for running a subset of tests ([ocaml/opam-repository#29166](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29166))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [Tibor](https://github.com/Geerayef) reported the broken Dream cookbook link and attempted a fix, but hit a `make switch` build failure where `topkg` referenced an unrelated local opam switch instead of the default one ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3703](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3703))

core: -Extensive back-and-forth on rewording the **"Signature mismatch" compiler error message**, weighing "subtype"/"supertype" terminology against readability and consistency with existing messages about `include`, involving [Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse), [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), and [Brandon Stride](https://github.com/brandonzstride) ([ocaml/ocaml#14896](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14896))

-[Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) pushed back on the **static/link-time linking proposal**, arguing that adding new `.cma` metadata fields and a shell-command-based `pkg-config` resolution mechanism needs a design document before merging, citing past problems with ad-hoc `META` file metadata and raising a security concern about archives embedding shell commands run at link time; [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) offered a MirageOS/Solo5 use case as a concrete test ([ocaml/ocaml#14895](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14895))

-Deep investigation into the **typing performance regression** in `js_of_ocaml`'s `lwt_xmlHttpRequest.ml`, with [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue), and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) diagnosing that unification of deeply nested object types causes repeated, non-shared expansion of type abbreviations ([ocaml/ocaml#14897](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14897), related to [ocaml/ocaml#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648) and [ocaml/ocaml#14801](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14801))

-[Simmo Saan](https://github.com/sim642) shared updated **Goblint benchmark results** comparing OCaml 4.14 against 5.6.0+trunk (including [ocaml/ocaml#14885](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14885) and [ocaml/ocaml#14894](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14894)), showing average CPU time regression down to +4% from earlier measurements, prompting [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) to ask about remaining outlier tests ([ocaml/ocaml#13733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13733))
ecosystem: -Contributors debated ownership of revdep breakage following the dune 3.24.0 release, with [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) asking whether fixing packages broken by a dune release falls to opam-repository maintainers, dune maintainers, or the affected package's own maintainer ([ocaml/opam-repository#30157](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30157))

-[Christophe Raffalli](https://github.com/craff) and [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) agreed to mark the old **simple_httpd** 1.0 as unavailable rather than resolve a disputed checksum ([ocaml/opam-repository#30123](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30123))

-[Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) tracked OCaml 5.5.0 release readiness ([ocaml/opam-repository#29463](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29463)), while a related macOS second-switch build failure for 5.5.0~alpha was reported by [@jrfondren](https://github.com/jrfondren) and traced to an upstream fix by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29496](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29496))

-[Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) asked the community to stop running `dune runtest` unconditionally in CI, with [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu) and [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) discussing existing mechanisms for running a subset of tests ([ocaml/opam-repository#29166](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29166))

-[Tibor](https://github.com/Geerayef) reported the broken Dream cookbook link and attempted a fix, but hit a `make switch` build failure where `topkg` referenced an unrelated local opam switch instead of the default one ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3703](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3703))

weekly_summary.emerging_trends:
- __RUMINANT:core__ A cluster of **typechecking and GC performance** work landed or progressed in ocaml/ocaml this week, including `deep_occur_rec` allocation removal ([ocaml/ocaml#14900](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14900)), ephemeron marking ([ocaml/ocaml#14688](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14688)), and ongoing Goblint/Frama-C regression tracking ([ocaml/ocaml#13733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13733), [ocaml/ocaml#11733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11733)), with [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) also reporting `-O3` and inlining-attribute experiments that cut `deep_occur_rec` time further
- __RUMINANT:core__ Maintainers are formalizing **process around AI-assisted contributions** to ocaml/ocaml, with [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)'s bug-report guideline proposal ([ocaml/ocaml#14899](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14899)) following earlier discussion in [ocaml/ocaml#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842)
- __RUMINANT:core__ A recurring pattern of **closure-allocation elimination in the typechecker** (renamings in recursive definitions from [ocaml/ocaml#14885](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14885)/[ocaml/ocaml#14894](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14894), and `deep_occur_rec` from [ocaml/ocaml#14900](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14900)) continues as a technique for compile-time speedups, motivated by real-world regressions reported by users of `js_of_ocaml` and Goblint
- __RUMINANT:core__ The single ocaml/RFCs proposal this week ([ocaml/RFCs#62](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/62)) continues a pattern of exposing compiler-internal mechanisms (here, destinations) to library and PPX authors rather than keeping them restricted to the compiler's internal IR
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Coordinated multi-package release rounds continued, exemplified by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm)'s MirageOS-ecosystem releases spanning x509, tls, charrua, mirage, and kdf within the same week ([ocaml/opam-repository#30154](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30154), [ocaml/opam-repository#30184](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30184))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ The dune 3.24.0 release ([ocaml/opam-repository#30092](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30092)) triggered downstream compatibility fixes across opam-repository and js_of_ocaml, and surfaced an open question about who owns fixing revdep breakage ([ocaml/opam-repository#30157](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30157))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Preparation for the OCaml 5.5.0 release continued in opam-repository, including a release-readiness tracking issue and a camlp5 fix for a test broken by 5.5.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29463](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29463), [ocaml/opam-repository#30163](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30163))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ ocaml.org content this week was driven mainly by recurring automated ingestion (Planet blog scraping, platform release tracking) rather than new manual authoring ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3702](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3702), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3693](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3693))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ Continued incremental hardening of the io_uring binding layer, with ocaml-uring 2.15.0 adding filesystem and socket operation coverage and raising the minimum supported OCaml version to 5.2 ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#157](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/157), [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/pull/144))
